obesity:GLP1R.verdict: Biology is de-risked. This program lives or dies on oral bioavailability and a differentiated tolerability profile, not on whether GLP-1R agonism works.
obesity:GLP1R.confidenceReason: Dense, directly analogous cohort: multiple approved GLP-1 programs, including one oral peptide.
obesity:GLP1R.cohortSummary: Of 6 mechanism-matched programs, 3 reached approval. The failures cluster not on efficacy but on oral delivery and tolerability.

cohort.reason: Approved with a SNAC absorption enhancer; ~1% oral bioavailability under strict fasting/water dosing.
Approved; sets the ~15% weight-loss efficacy benchmark.
Approved; established the class GI-tolerability profile.
Ongoing Phase 3; a non-peptide oral competitor raising the differentiation bar.
Discontinued after a liver-enzyme safety signal; illustrates oral GLP-1R tolerability/tox risk.
Modest efficacy; did not clear the class benchmark.

failureModes.title: Oral bioavailability collapse
failureModes.mechanism: Peptide is degraded and poorly absorbed in the GI tract; needs a permeation enhancer plus strict fasting/water conditions that crush real-world adherence.
failureModes.evidence: Oral semaglutide reaches only ~1% bioavailability with SNAC and strict dosing. A modest formulation shortfall drops exposure below the efficacious threshold.
failureModes.killExperiment: 14-day human PK bridging study of the formulation against the oral-semaglutide benchmark; measure the fed/fasted exposure ratio.

failureModes.title: GI tolerability ceiling
failureModes.mechanism: Nausea and vomiting limit up-titration to the efficacious dose; oral exposure spikes can worsen it versus steady injectable release.
failureModes.evidence: The whole GLP-1 class shows dose-limiting GI adverse events; discontinuation on titration is common.
failureModes.killExperiment: Phase 1 MAD with a slow-titration arm; track % discontinuation and the achieved tolerated dose.

failureModes.title: Efficacy differentiation vs a moving benchmark
failureModes.mechanism: The commercial bar is a 15–25% weight loss set by dual/triple agonists; a single-target oral GLP-1R may underwhelm even if it works.
failureModes.evidence: Approved and late-stage incretin combinations set a high threshold that GLP-1R monotherapy has not matched.
failureModes.killExperiment: Model achievable weight loss from Phase 1 exposure, then a 12-week Phase 2a signal versus historical control.

failureModes.title: Real-world adherence gap
failureModes.mechanism: Strict fasting and water conditions depress persistence, widening the gap between trial and market performance.
failureModes.evidence: Dosing conditions for oral peptides materially reduce real-world adherence.
failureModes.killExperiment: Patient-preference survey and adherence simulation against the injectable standard of care.

modality.verdict: Feasible but the hardest axis in the program. Precedent exists, but the formulation is the single largest technical risk.

axes.label: Proteolytic stability
axes.note: Native backbone is protease-labile; requires stabilizing modifications or enhancer co-formulation.

axes.label: Intestinal permeability
axes.note: Large hydrophilic peptide; a permeation enhancer (SNAC / C10) is essential and drives variability.

axes.label: Oral bioavailability
axes.note: Class precedent is ~1%; small formulation changes swing exposure widely.

axes.label: Target tissue access
axes.note: GLP-1R is peripherally accessible; no CNS penetration required for the metabolic effect.

axes.label: Manufacturing / CMC
axes.note: Solid-phase peptide synthesis at scale is established; enhancer co-formulation adds complexity.

bull: Mechanism is the most clinically validated in metabolic disease, GLP-1R agonism has multiple approvals across T2D and obesity.
A direct oral-peptide precedent exists (oral semaglutide), so the formulation and regulatory path is charted, not hypothetical.
Peripheral target: no blood-brain-barrier problem and no need for a novel biomarker to prove engagement.
bear: Every dollar of risk sits in formulation and tolerability, the two areas where oral peptides most often stall in Phase 1–2.
The efficacy benchmark is rising fast (dual/triple agonists at 20%+ weight loss); a single-target oral may be commercially undifferentiated even if it works.
Strict fasting/water dosing depresses real-world adherence, widening the gap between trial and market performance.

derisking.action: Run a 14-day human PK bridging study against the oral-semaglutide benchmark before any efficacy spend.
Add a slow-titration arm to first-in-human MAD to characterise the GI tolerability ceiling early.
Model achievable weight loss from Phase 1 exposure before committing to Phase 2b.

calibration.note: Metabolic-disease programs that read out after the 2019 cutoff; predictions use only pre-readout evidence.

obesity:MC4R.verdict: MC4R agonism works for weight, but broad (non-genetic) obesity has repeatedly failed on cardiovascular safety. Approvable only in a narrow genetic sub-population unless the safety window is solved.
obesity:MC4R.confidenceReason: Clear repeated precedent: approved narrowly (setmelanotide) and failed broadly on the same mechanism-linked toxicity.
obesity:MC4R.cohortSummary: 5 mechanism-matched programs. The single approval is confined to rare monogenic obesity; broad-obesity programs died in Phase 2 on the same cardiovascular signal.

cohort.reason: Approved only for rare POMC/LEPR/MC4R-pathway genetic obesity, not common obesity.
Blood-pressure and heart-rate increases at efficacious doses.
Insufficient weight-loss efficacy at tolerated doses.
Label-expansion program into acquired hypothalamic obesity.

failureModes.title: Cardiovascular safety (BP / heart rate)
failureModes.mechanism: MC4R agonism raises blood pressure and heart rate at doses needed for weight loss. This is mechanism-linked, not an off-target artefact.
failureModes.evidence: Multiple MC4R agonists showed the same BP/HR increase at efficacious exposure, a recurring, not idiosyncratic, death.
failureModes.killExperiment: Dedicated ambulatory-BP and cardiac-telemetry study in Phase 1 SAD/MAD at projected efficacious exposure.

failureModes.title: Efficacy only in a genetic sub-population
failureModes.mechanism: Robust weight loss reproduces in MC4R-pathway genetic obesity but is modest or absent in common polygenic obesity.
failureModes.evidence: The one approval is confined to monogenic obesity; broad-population efficacy has never convincingly reproduced.
failureModes.killExperiment: Enrich Phase 2 for MC4R-pathway variants with pre-specified genetic vs non-genetic arms.

failureModes.title: Narrow therapeutic index at a CNS target
failureModes.mechanism: Efficacy requires CNS exposure; on-target central effects (nausea, hyperpigmentation) narrow the usable window.
failureModes.evidence: On-target melanocortin effects appear close to the efficacious dose.
failureModes.killExperiment: PK/PD margin study measuring CNS adverse-event onset versus the weight-loss dose.

failureModes.title: Commercial differentiation
failureModes.mechanism: Enters a market where the GLP-1 class sets a 15–25% weight-loss bar that MC4R monotherapy has not approached broadly.
failureModes.evidence: Class benchmark far exceeds demonstrated MC4R broad-obesity efficacy.
failureModes.killExperiment: Target-product-profile gap analysis against the standard of care.

modality.verdict: Small-molecule access to MC4R is achievable. The constraint is biology and safety, not delivery.

axes.label: Target engagement
axes.note: Well-characterised GPCR; small-molecule agonists are tractable.

axes.label: CNS penetration
axes.note: Required for efficacy; adds toxicity surface.

axes.label: Melanocortin selectivity
axes.note: Off-target MC1R/MC3R/MC5R activity drives pigmentation and cardiovascular effects.

axes.label: Oral bioavailability
axes.note: Small molecule; standard oral PK is achievable.

axes.label: Therapeutic window
axes.note: Narrow: the efficacious dose overlaps the cardiovascular-effect dose.

bull: Mechanism is genetically validated, loss-of-function in the MC4R pathway causes severe obesity, so agonism is a human-validated lever.
One drug on this exact pathway is already approved (setmelanotide), proving the mechanism is drugged and regulable.
Small-molecule oral delivery is straightforward; there is no formulation risk.
bear: The cardiovascular failure is mechanism-linked and has killed multiple programs at the same step, a recurring, not idiosyncratic, death.
Efficacy convincingly reproduces only in a rare genetic population; the commercial prize (common obesity) is exactly where it has failed.
It enters a market where the GLP-1 class sets a 15–25% weight-loss bar that MC4R monotherapy has never approached.

derisking.action: Front-load an ambulatory-BP and cardiac-telemetry study at projected efficacious exposure in first-in-human.
Design Phase 2 with pre-specified genetic-enrichment vs common-obesity arms so the population question is answered, not confounded.
derisking.addresses: Efficacy only in a genetic sub-population

calibration.note: CNS and metabolic small-molecule programs; predictions use only pre-readout evidence.

alzheimers:BACE1.verdict: One of the most thoroughly invalidated approaches in the reference class. Every late-stage BACE1 inhibitor failed, several with dose-dependent cognitive worsening. Advancing another requires a different hypothesis, not a better molecule.
alzheimers:BACE1.confidenceReason: Large, unambiguous graveyard: six or more Phase 2/3 failures on the same target sharing one mechanism-linked failure mode.
alzheimers:BACE1.cohortSummary: 6 mechanism-matched programs. Five failed in Phase 2/3; several actively worsened cognition. The lone approval in the pathway used a different modality and mechanism.

cohort.reason: Futility; cognitive worsening versus placebo in the prodromal trial.
Halted in 2019 on an unfavourable risk–benefit balance.
Worsening cognition in a prevention cohort.
Approved, but via amyloid clearance, not BACE inhibition. The pathway yields a drug through a different modality.

failureModes.title: Mechanism-linked cognitive worsening
failureModes.mechanism: BACE1 has essential physiological substrates beyond APP (synaptic function). Inhibiting it produces dose-dependent cognitive decline versus placebo.
failureModes.evidence: Multiple independent BACE1 inhibitors produced the same cognitive-worsening signal, a property of the target, not the molecule.
failureModes.killExperiment: There is no cheap kill: this is a strategic stop. If pursued, a short high-dose cognitive-safety cohort surfaces it fast.

failureModes.title: Efficacy futility (wrong hypothesis / stage)
failureModes.mechanism: Amyloid reduction did not translate into clinical benefit in symptomatic AD; a timing and hypothesis mismatch.
failureModes.evidence: Deep BACE1-driven amyloid reduction repeatedly failed to move clinical endpoints.
failureModes.killExperiment: Biomarker-only Phase 1b showing amyloid/tau movement, though precedent says this will not predict clinical benefit.

failureModes.title: Hepatotoxicity
failureModes.mechanism: Several BACE1 inhibitors showed liver-enzyme elevations at therapeutic exposure.
failureModes.evidence: Atabecestat and others triggered LFT elevations.

modality.verdict: Delivery was never the problem. These molecules crossed the blood-brain barrier and hit the target. The mechanism is the failure.

axes.label: CNS penetration
axes.note: Achieved; brain BACE1 inhibition was demonstrated.

axes.label: Target engagement
axes.note: CSF amyloid reduction confirmed engagement in humans.

axes.label: BACE2 selectivity
axes.note: Off-target BACE2 effects (hypopigmentation) were observed.

axes.label: On-target safety
axes.note: BACE1 has essential physiological substrates; inhibition harms cognition.

axes.label: Oral bioavailability
axes.note: Oral CNS exposure was achievable.

bull: Target engagement was never in doubt, these molecules crossed the blood-brain barrier and measurably lowered amyloid.
The broader amyloid pathway did finally yield an approved drug (lecanemab), so the disease biology is partly tractable.
A large, well-documented failure record means the risks are known precisely, not speculatively.
bear: This is arguably the single most-invalidated target in the cohort: every late-stage program failed, and several worsened cognition.
The cognitive-worsening signal is mechanism-linked (BACE1's normal substrates), so a cleaner molecule does not obviously escape it.
The one pathway success came from a completely different modality and mechanism, arguing to change the approach, not the compound.

derisking.action: Do not advance a BACE1 inhibitor. Redirect to a validated pathway mechanism (amyloid clearance) or a genetically stronger target (APOE).
If pursued against advice, gate on a short cognitive-safety cohort before any efficacy investment.

calibration.note: Neurology / Alzheimer's programs; the AD graveyard makes high-risk predictions unusually well-calibrated.

obesity:GPR75.verdict: Human genetics is compelling, but the reference class is nearly empty. No target-matched program has read out. The honest output is a wide error bar, not a precise score.
obesity:GPR75.confidenceReason: Only two loosely-analogous programs exist and none has reached Phase 2; the model is extrapolating from genetics, not from outcomes.
obesity:GPR75.cohortSummary: The reference class is too thin to calibrate. This is a first-in-class bet resting on human loss-of-function genetics, not on comparable clinical outcomes.

cohort.reason: Early clinical; human LoF carriers are lean and healthy, supporting the target.
Class analog only; efficacy did not translate from preclinical models.

failureModes.title: Unknown on-target safety (novel biology)
failureModes.mechanism: No clinical safety readout exists for GPR75 modulation; this is genuine first-in-class risk.
failureModes.evidence: No precedent to borrow, the safety profile is entirely unobserved in humans.
failureModes.killExperiment: Standard first-in-human SAD/MAD safety with broad monitoring.

failureModes.title: Genetics-to-efficacy translation gap
failureModes.mechanism: Protective LoF variants suggest, but do not prove, that adult pharmacological antagonism reproduces the lean phenotype.
failureModes.evidence: Lifetime genetic protection is not equivalent to acute adult-onset pharmacology.
failureModes.killExperiment: Proof-of-mechanism biomarker plus a 12-week Phase 2a weight-loss signal.

failureModes.title: Thin competitive and reference data
failureModes.mechanism: Almost no comparable program has produced a clinical outcome, so any point estimate is low-confidence by construction.
failureModes.evidence: The reference class is nearly empty.
failureModes.killExperiment: Systematic competitive-landscape and patent scan to size white space and prior art.

modality.verdict: Small-molecule tractability for a GPCR is generally favourable; the uncertainty is biological, not about delivery.

axes.label: Target engagement
axes.note: Orphan GPCR; a tractable but less-characterised binding site.

axes.label: Oral bioavailability
axes.note: Small molecule; standard oral PK is plausible.

axes.label: Selectivity
axes.note: Orphan-receptor selectivity is unproven at the chemistry stage.

axes.label: Target tissue access
axes.note: Peripheral metabolic tissue access is likely sufficient.

axes.label: Biomarker availability
axes.note: No validated pharmacodynamic biomarker for engagement yet.

bull: Human loss-of-function carriers are lean and metabolically healthy, one of the strongest available forms of target validation.
First-in-class means open commercial space if it works.
Small-molecule tractability for a GPCR is generally favourable.
bear: The reference class is almost empty: no comparable program has produced a clinical outcome, so any point estimate is low-confidence by construction.
Lifetime genetic protection does not guarantee that adult-onset pharmacological antagonism reproduces the effect.
First-in-class safety is entirely unknown, there is no precedent to borrow.

derisking.action: Treat this as a value-of-information play: fund a tightly-scoped proof-of-mechanism package before betting on the point estimate.
Run a systematic competitive and patent scan to confirm white space before major spend.

calibration.note: Reference class too small to calibrate reliably. Scores are shown with a wide uncertainty band by design.
